competitors_analyzed.name: Northview Analytics

Five AI-adjacent open roles including MLOps Platform Engineer
CEO keynote at sector conference explicitly named agentic analytics as 2026 priority

Annual investor letter Q4 2025 positions AI platform as primary product differentiator

gap_findings.practice: Dedicated AI/ML leadership role at the executive level

peer_evidence.evidence: VP of AI named on public team page since Q4 2025.
Head of Applied AI hired November 2025 per LinkedIn.

gap_findings.prospect_state: Orrin Labs has no named AI/ML leadership role. CTO holds a combined infrastructure-and-data remit per the public team page.

gap_findings.practice: Dedicated MLOps / ML-platform engineering function (roles open 45+ days suggests active buildout)

peer_evidence.evidence: MLOps Platform Engineer role open and actively promoted.
Two MLOps Engineer roles open 45+ days.

gap_findings.prospect_state: Orrin Labs has Data Platform Engineer roles open but no explicitly MLOps-labeled roles. The distinction may be deliberate (integrated in data-platform role) or a gap.

gap_findings.practice: Public technical commentary on agentic or evaluation-framework work

peer_evidence.evidence: CTO podcast January 2026 discussing agentic workflows.
Technical blog post on evaluation framework, March 2026.

gap_findings.prospect_state: Orrin Labs blog mentions AI as a priority but has no technical depth posts on agentic workflows or evaluation in the last 12 months.

suggested_pitch_shift: Lead with the dedicated AI leadership gap (high confidence, two strong peer examples). Frame as a question — 'is the AI leadership role still being scoped, or is it deliberately distributed across the CTO remit?' — not an assertion of a gap. Avoid the MLOps gap in the first email; it is medium confidence and the prospect may have the function inside their Data Platform Engineer JD.
